title: Capitol Display Installation Records (“Thank You Governor Walz”)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records, documents, and communications generated, received, or maintained by your agency related to the display officially titled "Thank You Gov. Walz" as listed on its permit, which was located in the Minnesota State Capitol from April 2, 2025, through April 15, 2025. This includes records referring to the display by its official title ('Thank You Gov. Walz') or by descriptive references, including any communications containing language about a 'Democratic coalition of Satan worshippers.'

I request the following specific categories of records:

1. All communications (including emails, letters, text messages, or memos) regarding scheduling, setup, or removal of this display
2. Any records containing contact information for the individual(s) or entity that requested, applied for, or coordinated the display, or their representatives (including email addresses, phone numbers, or mailing addresses).
3. Any work orders, setup requests, or facilities coordination documents
4. Any incident reports, complaints, or internal discussions related to the display
5. Any records documenting any review of the display's adherence to applicable laws, regulations, or agency policies, including internal communications, legal opinions, and compliance assessments, excluding privileged materials.

This request excludes automated news alerts, media monitoring summaries, or bulk-distributed news digests unless they contain internal commentary.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rachel Cooper

title: Capitol Display Equipment Rental (“Thank You Governor Walz”)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records, documents, and communications generated, received, or maintained by your agency related to equipment rental associated with the display officially titled "Thank You Gov. Walz" as listed on its permit, which was located in the Minnesota State Capitol from April 2, 2025, through April 15, 2025. This includes records referring to the display by its official title ('Thank You Gov. Walz') or by descriptive references, including any communications containing language about a 'Democratic coalition of Satan worshippers.'

I request the following specific categories of records:

1. All signed agreements, contracts, or service authorizations covering equipment leased for the display.
2. All financial records including invoices, receipts, billing statements, transaction confirmations, and expense reports documenting costs incurred for the rental.
3. Documents specifying the person, department, or organization that initiated, authorized, or covered the rental expenses.
4. Any records containing contact information for the applicant(s) or their representatives (including email addresses, phone numbers, or mailing addresses).
5. All written exchanges, including email, letters, text messages, memos, or messaging platform communications, between agency personnel, applicants, and/or vendors concerning the rental logistics, scheduling, installation, or dismantling.
6. All internal agency communications, including emails, meeting minutes, briefing notes, or staff discussions, that reference the display or its associated equipment rental.

This request excludes automated news alerts, media monitoring summaries, or bulk-distributed news digests unless they contain internal commentary.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rachel Cooper

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: “Thank You Governor Walz” Capitol Display
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records, documents, and communications generated, received, or maintained by your agency relating to the display officially titled "Thank You Gov. Walz" as listed on its permit, which was located in the Minnesota State Capitol from April 2, 2025, through April 15, 2025. This includes records referring to the display by its official title ('Thank You Gov. Walz') or by descriptive references, including any communications containing language about a 'Democratic coalition of Satan worshippers.'

I request the following specific categories of records:

1. The complete permit application(s) and all attached exhibits, drafts, or supporting documentation.
2.  All communications (including emails, letters, text messages, or memos) between agency staff and the applicant(s), including any third parties involved in the design or placement.
3. Records of telephone communications related to this matter, including call logs, caller ID information, phone numbers, and any notes taken during or after such calls.
4. Any records containing contact information for the applicant(s) or their representatives (including email addresses, phone numbers, or mailing addresses).
5. Any notes, memos, legal opinions, or review documents created by agency staff regarding the content, placement, or compliance of the display.

This request excludes automated news alerts, media monitoring summaries, or bulk-distributed news digests unless they contain internal commentary.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

R Cooper
